players.name: Yazeed Abulaila
players.bio: Jordan's first-choice stopper with 76 caps, Abulaila is the calm hands behind Al-Nashama's defensive resilience. A loyal Al-Hussein servant, he's the veteran presence Jordan need for their historic World Cup debut.
players.facts: Has kept goal for Jordan for over a decade — and finally gets to do it on the biggest stage of all in 2026.

players.name: Mohammad Abu Hashish
players.bio: A dependable right-back with 56 caps and even a goal to his name, Abu Hashish brings experience and energy to Jordan's backline. His crossing and defensive discipline make him a reliable weapon down the flank.
players.facts: Defenders who score are rare — Abu Hashish has one international goal. He'll want more at a World Cup, naturally.

players.name: Abdallah Nasib
players.bio: A commanding centre-back with 65 caps and 3 international goals, Nasib is no stranger to danger — in either box. Plying his trade at Al-Zawraa in Iraq, he's battle-hardened and ready for the world stage.
players.facts: 3 goals in 65 caps as a defender — Nasib scores roughly as often as buses run on time. Remarkably reliable.

players.name: Husam Abu Dahab
players.bio: Young and ambitious, Abu Dahab is one of Jordan's rising defensive talents at just 26. With 18 caps under his belt, the Al-Faisaly man is earning his stripes and arrives at 2026 hungry to prove himself globally.
players.facts: Born on 13 May 2000 — a Taurus, which astrologers say makes him stubborn. Useful quality for a defender.

players.name: Yazan Al-Arab
players.bio: One of Jordan's most experienced outfield players with 80 caps, Al-Arab plies his trade in South Korea with FC Seoul. A cultured defender who has embraced life abroad, he's a true modern footballer.
players.facts: Al-Arab plays club football in Seoul, South Korea — making him one of Jordan's most well-travelled internationals.

players.name: Amer Jamous
players.full_name: Amer Rasem Adel Jamous; عَامِر رَاسِم عَادِل جَامُوس
players.bio: At just 23, Jamous is one of Jordan's most exciting young midfielders. Already with 19 caps and a goal to his name, the Al-Zawraa engine brings dynamism and ambition to the centre of the park.
players.facts: Born in 2002, Jamous was just 12 when Jordan last qualified for an Asian Cup final. Now he's at a World Cup.

players.name: Mohammad Abu Zrayq
players.full_name: Mohammad Faisal Yousef Abu Zrayq; محمد فيصل يوسف أبو زريق; Shararh
players.bio: Hailing from Ar-Ramtha, Abu Zrayq is Jordan's flying right winger who found his stage at Raja Casablanca. With 5 goals in 41 caps, the tricky wideman has the flair to trouble any full-back in the tournament.
players.facts: Abu Zrayq plays for Raja Casablanca — one of Africa's most storied clubs. Not bad for a lad from Ar-Ramtha.

players.name: Noor Al-Rawabdeh
players.full_name: Noor Al-Deen Mahmoud Ali Al-Rawabdeh; نُور الدِّيْن مَحمُود عَلِيّ الرَّوَّابدَة
players.bio: A energetic central midfielder with 68 caps, Al-Rawabdeh has built a career abroad with Selangor in Malaysia. He brings a blend of tenacity and technical ability to Jordan's engine room at his first World Cup.
players.facts: Al-Rawabdeh plays in Malaysia's Super League — giving Jordan a truly global squad spanning three continents.

players.name: Ali Olwan
players.full_name: Ali Iyad Ali Olwan; علي إياد علي علوان
players.bio: At just 26, Olwan is Jordan's main striker and one of their deadliest weapons — 29 goals in 66 caps is a remarkable return. Born in Amman, the Al-Sailiya forward will be the name opposition defences fear most.
players.facts: 29 goals in 66 caps gives Olwan a goals-per-game ratio that most strikers would envy. Jordan's golden boot hope.

players.name: Musa Al-Taamari
players.full_name: Musa Mohammad Sulaiman Al-Taamari; مُوسى مُحَمَّد مُوسى سُلَيْمَان التَعمري
players.bio: Jordan's star man and captain, Al-Taamari is the creative heartbeat of Al-Nashama. With 24 goals in 92 caps and a move to Rennes in Ligue 1, the Amman-born winger is Jordan's most recognisable football export.
players.facts: Al-Taamari wears the No.10 and plays in France's Ligue 1 — Jordan's answer to the classic trequartista superstar.

players.name: Odeh Al-Fakhouri
players.full_name: Odeh Fakhoury, Odeh Burhan Shehadeh Fakhoury, عودة برهان شحادة الفاخوري
players.bio: Born in 2005, Al-Fakhouri is a teenager stepping onto the grandest stage imaginable. Just 20 with 10 caps and already a goal scorer, the Pyramids winger is Jordan's most thrilling young talent.
players.facts: Al-Fakhouri was born the same year YouTube launched. He's that young — and already at a World Cup.

players.name: Nour Bani Attiah
players.full_name: Noureddin Zaid Khaleel Bani Ateyah; نُور الدِّين زِيَاد بَنِي عَطِيَّة
players.bio: Veteran backup goalkeeper Bani Attiah has waited patiently for his international chance, earning just 5 caps despite being 33. The Al-Faisaly stopper provides experienced cover and will relish the World Cup atmosphere.
players.facts: With only 5 caps at age 33, Bani Attiah is proof that patience is a virtue — especially for goalkeepers.

players.name: Mahmoud Al-Mardi
players.full_name: Mahmoud Nayef Mardi Al-Hwaitat; مَحْمُود نَايِف مَرْضِيّ الحويطات
players.bio: A veteran winger from Aqaba with 89 caps and 9 international goals, Al-Mardi is one of Jordan's most experienced performers. Quick, direct and dangerous, he's been a fixture on the left for over a decade.
players.facts: Al-Mardi is from Aqaba — Jordan's only sea port. He's been making waves for the national team ever since.

players.name: Rajaei Ayed
players.full_name: Rajaei Ayed Fadel Hasan; رجائي عايد فاضل حسن‎
players.bio: A tireless central midfielder and model of consistency, Ayed has 72 caps without a single international goal — clean sheets for the scorers, chaos for opponents. A dependable midfield anchor for Jordan's big moment.
players.facts: 72 caps, 0 goals. Rajaei Ayed is the ultimate selfless midfielder — all graft, no glory. And Jordan love him for it.

players.name: Ibrahim Sadeh
players.full_name: Ibrahim Mohammad Sami Sadeh; إبراهيم سعادة
players.bio: Born in Zarqa and developed at Al-Karma, Sadeh is a combative and creative central midfielder with 57 caps at 26. His three international goals suggest he's no stranger to arriving late in the box either.
players.facts: Sadeh was born in Zarqa — the same city as the legendary Abu Musab Al-Zarqawi. He's chosen a different path.

players.name: Mo Abualnadi
players.full_name: Mohammad Abu Al-Nadi; محمد ابو النادي
players.bio: Born in Overland Park, Kansas, Abualnadi is Jordan's slice of the American Midwest. The Selangor centre-back chose the Hashemites over the Stars and Stripes, adding international intrigue to his resume.
players.facts: Born in Kansas, plays in Malaysia, represents Jordan. Mo Abualnadi's football journey has more stamps than most passports.

players.name: Salim Obaid
players.bio: A defensive utility player with 11 caps, Obaid is one of the squad's older members at 34. His experience and professionalism make him a valued presence in a squad heading to their first-ever World Cup.
players.facts: At 34, Obaid is one of Jordan's oldest squad members — and has earned every grey hair waiting for this World Cup.

players.name: Mohammad Taha
players.full_name: Mohammad Ahmed Mohammad Taha; محمد أحمد محمد طه; Mohammad Abu Ghoush
players.bio: At just 20 with only 2 caps, Taha is a World Cup rookie in the truest sense. The young Al-Hussein left-back is one of Jordan's most exciting defensive prospects, getting the experience of a lifetime in 2026.
players.facts: Mohammad Taha has 2 caps. He's about to experience a World Cup. That's going from zero to hero very quickly.

players.name: Saed Al-Rosan
players.bio: A versatile defender with 21 caps and 2 goals, Al-Rosan provides energy and attacking intent from the backline. A dependable option for Jordan's coach, he's earned his place at the World Cup through consistent performances.
players.facts: Two goals in 21 caps as a defender — Al-Rosan clearly missed the memo that defenders aren't supposed to score.

players.name: Mohannad Abu Taha
players.bio: Still only 23, Abu Taha is a promising midfielder who plies his trade with the Iraqi side Al-Quwa Al-Jawiya. With 29 caps already banked, this composed playmaker has a bright future — starting at World Cup 2026.
players.facts: Abu Taha plays for Al-Quwa Al-Jawiya — the Iraqi Air Force club. His passing range is, fittingly, aerial.

players.name: Nizar Al-Rashdan
players.bio: A creative midfielder with 47 caps and 4 goals, Al-Rashdan operates out of Qatar SC with composure and craft. An important creative outlet for Jordan, his vision in tight spaces could be key in 2026.
players.facts: Al-Rashdan plays his club football in Qatar — he already knows what World Cup host-nation football looks like up close.

players.name: Abdallah Al-Fakhouri
players.bio: Back-up goalkeeper Al-Fakhouri provides solid competition between the sticks for Jordan. With 11 caps for the Al-Wehdat shot-stopper, he's a trusted second option who keeps the No.1 on their toes.
players.facts: Al-Fakhouri shares a surname with winger Odeh Al-Fakhouri (No.11). Jordan's squad — practically a family reunion.

players.name: Ihsan Haddad
players.bio: One of Jordan's most capped players with 92 appearances, Haddad is a rock-solid defender who has given everything for Al-Nashama. At 32, this World Cup represents the crowning achievement of a distinguished career.
players.facts: 92 caps and still going — Ihsan Haddad has more Jordan appearances than almost anyone. A true Al-Nashama legend.

players.name: Ali Azaizeh
players.bio: A 22-year-old forward with Al-Shabab, Azaizeh is one of the fresher faces in Jordan's 2026 squad. With just 4 caps, he's a wildcard option up front — young, hungry, and desperate to make his mark.
players.facts: Four caps at 22 — Azaizeh is still writing chapter one of his international story. What better place than a World Cup?

players.name: Mohammad Al-Dawoud
players.bio: An experienced Al-Wehdat midfielder at 34, Al-Dawoud brings wisdom and craft to Jordan's midfield options. With 13 caps and a World Cup spot secured, he proves it's never too late to reach the pinnacle of the game.
players.facts: Al-Dawoud is 34 and heading to his first World Cup. Proof that dreams don't have an expiry date.

players.name: Anas Badawi
players.bio: With just 1 cap to his name, Badawi is perhaps Jordan's most uncapped squad member — but the Al-Faisaly defender has earned his spot through domestic excellence. The World Cup dream is real, even for late bloomers.
players.facts: Anas Badawi has 1 international cap. He'll spend the World Cup hoping cap number 2 is just around the corner.
